root_text.an4.243:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kosambiyaṁ viharati ghositārāme.
root_text.an4.243:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā ānando yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.an4.243:1.5: Āyasmato, bhante, anuruddhassa bāhiyo nāma saddhivihāriko kevalakappaṁ saṅghabhedāya ṭhito.
root_text.an4.243:1.6: Tatrāyasmā anuruddho na ekavācikampi bhaṇitabbaṁ maññatī”ti.
root_text.an4.243:2.2: Nanu, ānanda, yāni kānici adhikaraṇāni uppajjanti, sabbāni tāni tumhe ceva vūpasametha sāriputtamoggallānā ca.
root_text.an4.243:3.1: Cattārome, ānanda, atthavase sampassamāno pāpabhikkhu saṅghabhedena nandati.
root_text.an4.243:3.3: Idhānanda, pāpabhikkhu dussīlo hoti pāpadhammo asuci saṅkassarasamācāro paṭicchannakammanto assamaṇo samaṇapaṭiñño abrahmacārī brahmacāripaṭiñño antopūti avassuto kasambujāto.
root_text.an4.243:3.6: dussīlo pāpadhammo asuci saṅkassarasamācāro paṭicchannakammanto assamaṇo samaṇapaṭiñño abrahmacārī brahmacāripaṭiñño antopūti avassuto kasambujātoti, samaggā maṁ santā nāsessanti;
root_text.an4.243:3.8: Idaṁ, ānanda, paṭhamaṁ atthavasaṁ sampassamāno pāpabhikkhu saṅghabhedena nandati.
root_text.an4.243:4.1: Puna caparaṁ, ānanda, pāpabhikkhu micchādiṭṭhiko hoti, antaggāhikāya diṭṭhiyā samannāgato.
root_text.an4.243:4.4: micchādiṭṭhiko antaggāhikāya diṭṭhiyā samannāgatoti, samaggā maṁ santā nāsessanti;
root_text.an4.243:4.6: Idaṁ, ānanda, dutiyaṁ atthavasaṁ sampassamāno pāpabhikkhu saṅghabhedena nandati.
root_text.an4.243:5.1: Puna caparaṁ, ānanda, pāpabhikkhu micchāājīvo hoti, micchāājīvena jīvikaṁ kappeti.
root_text.an4.243:5.4: micchāājīvo micchāājīvena jīvikaṁ kappetīti, samaggā maṁ santā nāsessanti;
root_text.an4.243:5.6: Idaṁ, ānanda, tatiyaṁ atthavasaṁ sampassamāno pāpabhikkhu saṅghabhedena nandati.
root_text.an4.243:6.1: Puna caparaṁ, ānanda, pāpabhikkhu lābhakāmo hoti sakkārakāmo anavaññattikāmo.
root_text.an4.243:6.4: lābhakāmo sakkārakāmo anavaññattikāmoti, samaggā maṁ santā na sakkarissanti na garuṁ karissanti na mānessanti na pūjessanti;
root_text.an4.243:6.5: vaggā pana maṁ sakkarissanti garuṁ karissanti mānessanti pūjessantī’ti.
root_text.an4.243:6.6: Idaṁ, ānanda, catutthaṁ atthavasaṁ sampassamāno pāpabhikkhu saṅghabhedena nandati.
root_text.an4.243:6.7: Ime kho, ānanda, cattāro atthavase sampassamāno pāpabhikkhu saṅghabhedena nandatī”ti.

translation_text.an4.243:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Kosambī, in Ghosita’s Monastery.
translation_text.an4.243:1.2: Then Venerable Ānanda went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him,
translation_text.an4.243:1.3: “Well, Ānanda, has that disciplinary issue been settled yet?”
translation_text.an4.243:1.5: Venerable Anuruddha’s protégé Bāhiya remains entirely committed to creating a schism in the Saṅgha.
translation_text.an4.243:1.6: But Anuruddha doesn’t think to say a single word about it.”
translation_text.an4.243:2.1: “But Ānanda, since when has Anuruddha been involved in disciplinary issues in the midst of the Saṅgha?
translation_text.an4.243:2.2: Shouldn’t you, together with Sāriputta and Moggallāna, settle all disciplinary issues that come up?
translation_text.an4.243:3.1: A bad monk sees four reasons to relish schism in the Saṅgha.
translation_text.an4.243:3.3: Take an unethical monk, of bad qualities, filthy, with suspicious behavior, underhand, no true ascetic or spiritual practitioner—though claiming to be one—rotten inside, festering, and depraved.
translation_text.an4.243:3.5: ‘Suppose the monks know that I’m a bad monk …
translation_text.an4.243:3.6: If they’re in harmony, they’ll expel me,
translation_text.an4.243:3.7: but if they’re divided they won’t.’
translation_text.an4.243:3.8: A bad monk sees this as the first reason to relish schism in the Saṅgha.
translation_text.an4.243:4.1: Furthermore, a bad monk has wrong view, he’s attached to an extremist view.
translation_text.an4.243:4.3: ‘Suppose the monks know that I have wrong view …
translation_text.an4.243:4.4: If they’re in harmony they’ll expel me,
translation_text.an4.243:4.5: but if they’re divided they won’t.’
translation_text.an4.243:4.6: A bad monk sees this as the second reason to relish schism in the Saṅgha.
translation_text.an4.243:5.1: Furthermore, a bad monk has wrong livelihood and earns a living by wrong livelihood.
translation_text.an4.243:5.3: ‘Suppose the monks know that I have wrong livelihood …
translation_text.an4.243:5.4: If they’re in harmony they’ll expel me,
translation_text.an4.243:5.5: but if they’re divided they won’t.’
translation_text.an4.243:5.6: A bad monk sees this as the third reason to relish schism in the Saṅgha.
translation_text.an4.243:6.1: Furthermore, a bad monk desires material things, honor, and admiration.
translation_text.an4.243:6.3: ‘Suppose the monks know that I desire material things, honor, and admiration.
translation_text.an4.243:6.4: If they’re in harmony they won’t honor, respect, revere, or venerate me,
translation_text.an4.243:6.5: but if they’re divided they will.’
translation_text.an4.243:6.6: A bad monk sees this as the fourth reason to relish schism in the Saṅgha.
translation_text.an4.243:6.7: A bad monk sees these four reasons to relish schism in the Saṅgha.”
